Stub functions for initializing and handling various analytics metrics and events in PRF.

# Purpose
The code is a C source file that provides stub implementations for a set of analytics functions. These functions are part of an analytics module, as indicated by the inclusion of the header file `services/common/analytics/analytics.h`. The file defines a series of functions that are intended to track and report various analytics metrics and events. These functions include [`analytics_init`](<#analytics_init>), [`analytics_set`](<#analytics_set>), [`analytics_max`](<#analytics_max>), [`analytics_inc`](<#analytics_inc>), and [`analytics_add`](<#analytics_add>), which are used to initialize analytics, set and update metrics, and perform operations like incrementing or adding values to metrics.

Additionally, the file contains functions for handling specific events, such as [`analytics_event_app_oom`](<#analytics_event_app_oom>), [`analytics_event_app_launch`](<#analytics_event_app_launch>), and [`analytics_event_bt_connection_or_disconnection`](<#analytics_event_bt_connection_or_disconnection>). These functions are designed to log events related to application out-of-memory conditions, application launches, and Bluetooth connection or disconnection events, among others. The functions are currently implemented as stubs, meaning they do not contain any operational code. This suggests that the file serves as a placeholder or template for future development, where the actual logic for analytics tracking and event handling will be implemented.

Handles an out-of-memory event by recording relevant memory statistics.
